name: gpt-oss-120b (High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-experts transformer, 128 experts/layer, top-4 active, 36 layers
note: OpenAI's first open-weight model since GPT-2; 117B MoE (5.1B active), Apache 2.0.
tpsNote: Does not fit on a single RTX 4090 (24GB); OpenAI specifies a single 80GB GPU (H100/MI300X) for native MXFP4 inference.

results.notes: AIME 2024, no tools, high reasoning effort
GPQA Diamond, no tools, high reasoning effort
HLE, no tools, high reasoning effort. Read directly from Table 3 ('Evaluations across multiple benchmarks and reasoning levels') of the official gpt-oss-120b & gpt-oss-20b Model Card PDF.
Tau-Bench Retail, high reasoning effort. Read directly from Table 3 of the official model card.
Aider Polyglot, high reasoning effort. Read directly from Table 3 of the official model card.
MMMLU average across 14 human-translated languages, high reasoning effort. Read directly from Table 2/Table 3 of the official model card.

Codeforces Elo, no terminal tool, high reasoning effort (2622 Elo with tool access, per the same table -- chose the no-tools figure for consistency with the model's other no-tools rows). Read directly from Table 3 of the official model card.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 26/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "gpt-oss-120b" (OpenAI). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. Attached to the High reasoning-effort sibling of gpt-oss-120b (highest available).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real. Same HF/architecture verification as other effort variants. Fetched arxiv.org/html/2508.10925v1 table directly and confirmed all four high-effort scores appear verbatim: AIME 95.8, GPQA 80.1, MMLU 90.0, SWE-bench Verified 62.4. No changes made.

2026-07-16: Researched the four missing core benchmarks (math/MATH-500, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) against the official model card (arXiv 2508.10925, full Table 3 fetched via arxiv.org/html/2508.10925v1), independent leaderboards (Vals.ai model page for 'GPT OSS 120B', Artificial Analysis, Epoch AI), an independent academic evaluation paper (arXiv 2508.12461, 'Is GPT-OSS Good?'), and all five gpt-oss sibling family files (120b-low/medium, 20b-low/medium/high). MATH-500: not reported by OpenAI (Table 3 covers math only via AIME 2024/2025, already tracked) and absent from every independent leaderboard checked; no sibling file has it either -- genuine absence, omitted_no_evidence. HumanEval: not in the official card; arXiv 2508.12461 reports a HumanEval=71% figure for gpt-oss-120b but specifies no reasoning-effort tier and pairs it with an MMLU=66% reading wildly inconsistent with OpenAI's own 85.9-90% MMLU range across all three effort tiers, indicating a non-standard/non-comparable harness -- too unreliable to attribute to this High-effort file, omitted_no_evidence. LiveCodeBench: not in the official card; Vals.ai's independent page for 'GPT OSS 120B' (vals.ai/models/fireworks_gpt-oss-120b, fetched directly and re-confirmed on a second load) does show a real LiveCodeBench score of 83.23% (+/-1.05, rank 37/127), but Vals.ai's own stated methodology runs 'the default configuration given by the provider,' and OpenAI's documented API default reasoning effort for gpt-oss models is Medium, not High -- attributing this number to the High-effort file would risk the variant-conflation error flagged in prior corpus audits, so it is deliberately left out here (it more plausibly belongs on the -medium.json sibling, which is isDefaultConfig:true, for a future pass). MMMU: gpt-oss-120b is text-only (capabilities.vision=false per this file and confirmed by OpenAI's own launch materials), so this vision/multimodal benchmark is structurally inapplicable rather than merely unpublished -- omitted_no_evidence.

2026-07-18: Round-22 targeted re-search for the two flagged current-gen gaps (livecodebench, mmmu), chasing all 6 prescribed methods before concluding absence.

LIVECODEBENCH -- exhausted all 6 methods, still no citable High-effort-specific number:
(1) Official model card: re-fetched full HTML of arXiv 2508.10925 (arxiv.org/html/2508.10925v1) and text-searched for "LiveCodeBench" -- zero occurrences anywhere in the document (only MMMLU, a different benchmark, is present). Confirms the 2026-07-16 finding.
(2) Independent trackers: Artificial Analysis's current Intelligence Index v4.1 page for gpt-oss-120b (high) (artificialanalysis.ai/models/gpt-oss-120b -- page title verbatim confirms "(high)") lists its 9 component evals (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR); LiveCodeBench is not one of them under the current methodology. AA's own dedicated LiveCodeBench leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/livecodebench) was fetched and text-searched for "gpt-oss" -- zero matches. A third-party aggregator (benchlm.ai/models/gpt-oss-120b) cites "AA LiveCodeBench: 87.8%" attributed to "Artificial Analysis: livecodebench leaderboard," and a user on OpenAI's community forum (community.openai.com/t/test-gpt-oss-120b-on-livecodebench/1367618) references this same 87.8 as the "high setting" figure -- but it could not be reproduced or located on AA's live site (directly contradicted by the leaderboard search above), so it lacks a verifiable, stable sourceUrl and was not added. Vals.ai (vals.ai/models/fireworks_gpt-oss-120b) shows ~83.2% on LiveCodeBench, but per its own stated methodology this runs "the default configuration given by the provider" (Medium for gpt-oss, per OpenAI's documented API default) -- already correctly withheld from this High-effort file in the 2026-07-16 pass, re-confirmed here. Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/gpt-oss-120b) reports only its own Epoch Capabilities Index (ECI=141, rank 79/211) and no LiveCodeBench score. llm-stats.com's gpt-oss-120b page has a LiveCodeBench nav entry but shows only a "Loading chart..." placeholder with no populated data.
(3) Academic sources: arXiv 2508.12461 ("Is GPT-OSS Good?") evaluates coding only via HumanEval/MBPP, no LiveCodeBench. arXiv 2606.20517 ("Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages") does test GPT-OSS-120B, but its results table (Table 1) reports only Medium (67.8% Pass@1 avg, Feb-May 2025 v6 tasks) and Low (53.1%) reasoning effort -- High is entirely absent from their evaluation.
(4) GitHub eval-harness logs: MLCommons MLPerf Inference v6.0 added an official gpt-oss-120b benchmark (github.com/mlcommons/inference, language/gpt-oss-120b) whose accuracy-mode reference run does use LiveCodeBench v6 as one of three constituent datasets (with AIME25 and GPQA Diamond) at reasoning_effort=high -- but the published reference score (83.13%, 99%-of-reference target threshold 82.30%) is a single BLENDED accuracy across all three datasets combined; the README does not break out a per-dataset LiveCodeBench-only figure, so it cannot honestly be attributed as "the LiveCodeBench score" without misrepresenting a multi-benchmark composite as a single-benchmark result.
(5) Sibling-model comparison: gpt-oss-20b's own model card (same arXiv 2508.10925 paper) likewise omits LiveCodeBench entirely, so no same-family launch table offers a usable comparison figure.
(6) HF discussion (huggingface.co/openai/gpt-oss-120b/discussions/100) and the OpenAI community thread contain only unofficial, self-run local-deployment numbers with materially inconsistent methodology (mixed LCB v5/v6 versions, different problem subsets, quantization/inference-framework choices) -- the same poster's own repeated High-effort attempts landed at 60%, 61%, and 62% across different tool setups, an inconsistency spread that itself disqualifies these as a reliable citable number, and their own reference to an "87.8 on high" AA figure could not be independently corroborated (per point 2).
Conclusion: genuinely not yet published by any source meeting this corpus's citability bar for the High-effort variant specifically -- omitted_no_evidence stands, not merely unsearched.

MMMU -- re-confirmed architecturally inapplicable: this file's own capabilities.vision=false and modalities=["text"] fields are corroborated by Artificial Analysis's technical-specifications block for gpt-oss-120b (high) ("Input modality: Supports: text" / "Output modality: Supports: text") and by the official model card (arXiv 2508.10925), which contains no vision-benchmark section at all. gpt-oss-120b is a text-only MoE LLM with no image encoder or vision-language training; MMMU (an image+text multimodal benchmark) cannot be meaningfully administered to it. Same conclusion as the 2026-07-16 pass; omitted_no_evidence stands, reason: architecturally inapplicable (text-only model).
